track.title: SACCO Smart Member
track.description: A beginner route for SACCO membership, deposits, share capital, dividends, rebates, loan multipliers, guarantor risk, and status checks.

course.title: SACCO Smart Member: Core route
course.description: Understand SACCO rules before joining or increasing contributions.

lessons.title: What is a SACCO?
lessons.summary: A SACCO is a member-owned cooperative that can support saving, borrowing, and member services.
lessons.introduction: A SACCO is a member-owned cooperative. It can help members save, access loans, and share returns, but each SACCO has rules on membership, deposits, share capital, loans, guarantors, and exit.
sections: A SACCO decision is partly financial and partly governance-based.
Members should read bylaws, audited accounts where available, loan rules, and exit rules.
Regulation and supervision differ by SACCO type, so public status checks matter.

scenario.title: New member question
scenario.text: A worker is invited to join a SACCO. Before paying, they ask how deposits, share capital, dividends, loan eligibility, and withdrawals work.

lessons.mistake: Joining because friends joined without checking rules and status.
lessons.action: Write the SACCO name, regulated status check, joining cost, and exit rule.
lessons.takeaway: A smart SACCO member reads the rules before needing them.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify regulated SACCO lists using SASRA public pages where applicable.

lessons.title: Deposits vs share capital
lessons.summary: Deposits and share capital are not the same and may have different access rules.
lessons.introduction: SACCO deposits and share capital can serve different purposes. A member should know what can be withdrawn, what may be locked, what earns returns, and what supports membership rights.
sections: Deposits are often linked to savings and loan eligibility, but withdrawal rules vary.
Share capital may represent ownership contribution and can have stricter exit rules.
A contribution can feel like savings but behave differently under bylaws.

scenario.title: Exit confusion
scenario.text: A member assumes all contributions can be withdrawn quickly, then learns share capital follows a separate process.

lessons.mistake: Calling every contribution savings without checking whether it is withdrawable.
lessons.action: Ask the SACCO to separate deposits, share capital, fees, and other deductions in writing.
lessons.takeaway: The label on a contribution determines access and expectations.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify SACCO-specific rules directly with the SACCO and public regulator sources where available.

lessons.title: Dividends and interest rebates
lessons.summary: Dividends and rebates depend on SACCO performance, rules, and approval processes.
lessons.introduction: SACCO members may hear about dividends or interest rebates. These are not automatic personal promises. They depend on the SACCO's performance, rules, approvals, and member balances.
sections: Dividend history can help with learning but should not be treated as next year's result.
Interest rebates may relate to borrowing activity and SACCO policy.
Members should ask how returns are calculated, approved, and communicated.

scenario.title: Dividend expectation
scenario.text: A member joins mainly because last year's dividend was attractive. They pause to ask how the figure was calculated and whether it can change.

lessons.mistake: Joining only because of last year's payout story.
lessons.action: Ask for the latest annual report or member communication and write what is uncertain.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Use SACCO documents and SASRA public information where applicable.

lessons.title: Loan multipliers and guarantor risk
lessons.summary: Loan access can create obligations for both borrower and guarantor.
lessons.introduction: Many SACCOs link loan eligibility to deposits or member contributions. Loan multipliers can be useful, but borrowing rules and guarantor obligations must be understood before anyone signs.
sections: A loan multiplier is not free money. It is debt under SACCO rules.
A guarantor may carry real exposure if the borrower fails to meet obligations.
Members should understand default rules, security requirements, and how guarantor release works.

scenario.title: Guarantor request
scenario.text: A friend asks you to guarantee a loan. You ask for the amount, repayment plan, your exposure, and what happens if they default.

lessons.mistake: Guaranteeing because of friendship without knowing the liability.
lessons.action: Write the maximum amount you could be exposed to before agreeing to guarantee.
lessons.takeaway: Loan access is useful only when obligations are clear.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify guarantor and loan rules directly with the SACCO.

lessons.title: How to check SACCO status
lessons.summary: Use public lists, official documents, and direct questions before joining.
lessons.introduction: Before joining or increasing contributions, check whether the SACCO is regulated or listed where applicable, and ask for official documents. Verification is part of member protection.
sections: Use SASRA public lists for deposit-taking and regulated SACCO categories where applicable.
Ask the SACCO for bylaws, member terms, fees, audited accounts, and withdrawal rules.
Avoid sending money to informal contacts without official payment instructions.

scenario.title: Name similarity risk
scenario.text: A user sees a SACCO-like name online. They verify the exact name and official contacts before sending any joining fee.

lessons.mistake: Assuming a familiar name proves current status.
lessons.action: Compare the exact SACCO name against public lists and official documents.
lessons.takeaway: Verification should happen before payment, not after a problem.
lessons.source_note: Source note: SASRA public lists should be checked for applicable SACCO categories.

lessons.title: SACCO simulator practice
lessons.summary: Use the simulator to understand contributions, projected dividends, and access trade-offs.
lessons.introduction: The SACCO simulator is an education tool for contributions and possible dividend assumptions. It cannot know governance quality, future approvals, loan defaults, or actual SACCO performance.
sections: Try changing monthly contribution to see the savings discipline effect.
Try changing dividend assumption to see sensitivity.
Write the rules the simulator cannot answer, such as exit timing and guarantor exposure.

scenario.title: Monthly contribution
scenario.text: A member tests KES 3,000 per month for a year and records what still needs verification.

lessons.mistake: Treating a projection as a SACCO promise.
lessons.action: Run one scenario and write two questions for the SACCO office.
lessons.takeaway: The simulator helps you ask better questions.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify actual SACCO terms and returns directly with the SACCO.

options: Possible exposure to another member's loan obligation
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Possible exposure to another member's loan obligation
quiz_questions.explanation: A guarantor may carry real liability under SACCO rules.

quiz_questions.prompt: Why separate deposits from share capital?
options: They may have different access and exit rules
quiz_questions.correct_answer: They may have different access and exit rules
quiz_questions.explanation: Different contribution labels can behave differently.

quiz_questions.prompt: When should SACCO status be checked?

flashcards.back: A member-owned cooperative that can support saving, borrowing, and member services.
flashcards.example: Members contribute and follow SACCO rules.

flashcards.back: It can represent ownership contribution and may have stricter exit rules.
flashcards.example: It may not withdraw like ordinary savings.

flashcards.back: No. They depend on performance, rules, and approvals.
A loan eligibility rule linked to deposits or contributions.
flashcards.example: It is still debt, not free money.

flashcards.back: Exact SACCO name, status where applicable, bylaws, fees, and withdrawal rules.
flashcards.example: Check SASRA lists for relevant categories.

resources.title: SACCO joining checklist
resources.body: A joining checklist for SACCO status, bylaws, deposits, share capital, fees, loan rules, guarantor exposure, audited accounts, and exit timelines.
items: Verify exact SACCO status where applicable.
Ask for bylaws and fee schedule.
